title: LO3 — Tradeoff debate: approach A vs approach B from Unit 3
body:
Unit 3 presents two competing approaches to the same problem and asks us to evaluate their tradeoffs. I want to make sure I'm not missing anything before the assessment.

My current read:
- Approach A is simpler to implement and reason about, but has worse worst-case behaviour.
- Approach B has better guarantees but requires a non-trivial invariant to hold at all times, which adds implementation risk.

The lecture seemed to favour Approach B for production contexts. Does that match what others took away? Are there cases where A is clearly the better choice?
author: student

comments.author: user-1
comments.body: I agree with what student said. Approach B does seem better for production. I also think Approach A has its place in simpler contexts.

comments.author: admin
comments.body: I also agree. The lecture made this point clearly. Approach B is the right choice when correctness guarantees matter.

comments.author: student2
comments.body: Same here, I agree with the points above. Approach A seems simpler but Approach B is better in most cases from what I can tell.

comments.author: student3
comments.body: I read the same thing in the slides. I agree that Approach B is preferred. Good summary student.

comments.author: student4
comments.body: I also agree with what was said. The tradeoff is clear once you read the lecture notes carefully.
